item_a.name: LG C3 OLED TV
item_a.image_alt: LG C3 OLED TV displaying vibrant colors on screen with slim profile and minimalist design
pros: Superior brightness uniformity and color accuracy with perfect black levels
Comprehensive gaming features with 4 HDMI 2.1 ports and G-Sync/FreeSync support
WebOS 23 interface is intuitive and feature-rich with extensive app support
Better viewing angles maintain color accuracy from wider positions
cons: Lower peak brightness at 800 nits compared to QD-OLED competition
More susceptible to burn-in with static content over time
Higher input lag in certain picture modes affects competitive gaming
item_a.best_for: Home theater enthusiasts who prioritize accurate colors, perfect blacks, and wide viewing angles for mixed content consumption.

item_b.name: Samsung S90C QD-OLED TV
item_b.image_alt: Samsung S90C QD-OLED TV showcasing quantum dot technology with bright HDR content and modern stand design
pros: Significantly brighter with 1,500 nits peak brightness for superior HDR impact
Quantum Dot OLED technology delivers more vivid and saturated colors
Near-instantaneous response time of 0.1ms ideal for gaming and fast sports
Tizen OS offers seamless Samsung ecosystem integration and Gaming Hub
cons: Only 2 full-bandwidth HDMI 2.1 ports limits connectivity for multiple gaming devices
Viewing angles show color shift and washing out off-center more than WOLED
Aggressive auto-brightness limiter can reduce brightness in certain scenes
item_b.best_for: Gamers and HDR movie fans who watch in controlled lighting and want the brightest, most vibrant OLED picture quality available.

comparison_summary: The LG C3 vs Samsung S90C debate centers on WOLED versus QD-OLED technology. The Samsung S90C delivers dramatically brighter images with more saturated colors thanks to quantum dots, making it exceptional for HDR content and bright room viewing, while the LG C3 offers superior viewing angles, better color accuracy, and more gaming connectivity with four HDMI 2.1 ports. Both are premium OLED displays with near-perfect contrast, but they excel in different scenarios.
verdict_text: The Samsung S90C edges out the LG C3 for most buyers thanks to its substantially brighter QD-OLED panel that delivers more impactful HDR and better daytime viewing, though the C3 remains the better choice for wider seating arrangements and those needing four HDMI 2.1 ports.

key_differences.item_b: Color shift and brightness loss beyond 45° horizontally
Tizen OS with Solar Cell Remote

seo_content:
The LG C3 vs Samsung S90C comparison represents one of the most compelling television debates of 2024-2025, pitting two different OLED technologies against each other at similar price points. Both displays offer the infinite contrast ratio and perfect blacks that define OLED technology, but they achieve their picture quality through fundamentally different approaches that create distinct viewing experiences.

When comparing the Samsung S90C vs LG C3, the most immediately noticeable difference is brightness. Samsung's QD-OLED panel technology combines quantum dots with an OLED emissive layer, allowing the S90C to reach peak brightness levels around 1,500 nits in HDR content—nearly double the C3's 800 nits. This brightness advantage translates to more impactful HDR highlights, better visibility in bright rooms, and colors that pop with exceptional vibrancy. If you primarily watch during daytime or have significant ambient light, the S90C's brightness superiority becomes a decisive factor. The quantum dot layer also produces more saturated colors, particularly in reds and greens, creating a more visually striking picture that some viewers prefer for movies and gaming.

The LG C3, however, counters with its own strengths rooted in traditional WOLED technology. Its viewing angles are substantially better, maintaining color accuracy and brightness consistency when viewed from the side—critical for larger living rooms where family members sit at various angles. The C3 also includes four full-bandwidth HDMI 2.1 ports compared to the S90C's two, making it more practical for users with multiple gaming consoles, PCs, or external devices. LG's implementation of Dolby Vision gaming and comprehensive VRR support across all HDMI ports gives it an edge for serious gaming setups.

Color accuracy is another consideration in the LG C3 vs Samsung S90C decision. The C3 delivers more accurate colors out of the box for content creators and cinema purists who want images that match director intent. The S90C's quantum dot colors are more vivid but can appear oversaturated to trained eyes, though most casual viewers find them more appealing.

For gaming, both televisions excel with sub-1ms response times, but the S90C's 0.1ms response and higher brightness give it a slight advantage in competitive scenarios. Meanwhile, the C3's extra HDMI 2.1 ports and slightly lower input lag in Game Optimizer mode make it more versatile for multi-console households.

Price positioning varies by screen size and sales timing, but both typically range from $1,300 to $2,600 depending on whether you're buying the 55-inch, 65-inch, or 77-inch model. The S90C generally commands a $100-200 premium when comparing identical sizes, reflecting its newer QD-OLED technology, though aggressive sales can flip this pricing dynamic.

Ultimately, the Samsung S90C vs LG C3 choice depends on your viewing environment and priorities. The S90C wins for brightness-focused viewers who want the most impressive HDR and watch primarily from center seating positions, while the C3 better serves wider seating arrangements and connectivity-demanding setups. Both represent excellent OLED televisions that will satisfy the vast majority of buyers.

faq.q: Which is brighter, the LG C3 or Samsung S90C?
faq.a: The Samsung S90C is significantly brighter, achieving approximately 1,500 nits peak brightness compared to the LG C3's 800 nits. This makes the S90C better for bright rooms and more impactful HDR content, while the C3 performs better in controlled lighting environments.

faq.q: Does the LG C3 or Samsung S90C have better viewing angles?
faq.a: The LG C3 has substantially better viewing angles, maintaining color accuracy and brightness beyond 70 degrees horizontally. The Samsung S90C's QD-OLED panel shows more color shift and brightness loss when viewed from angles beyond 45 degrees, making it less ideal for wide seating arrangements.

faq.q: How many HDMI 2.1 ports do the LG C3 and Samsung S90C have?
faq.a: The LG C3 has four full-bandwidth HDMI 2.1 ports, while the Samsung S90C has only two (HDMI ports 3 and 4). This gives the C3 a significant advantage for users with multiple gaming consoles, PCs, or 4K 120Hz devices who want them all connected simultaneously.

faq.q: Which TV is better for gaming, the LG C3 or Samsung S90C?
faq.a: Both are excellent for gaming with sub-1ms response times and VRR support. The Samsung S90C has a slight edge with 0.1ms response time and higher brightness for HDR gaming, while the LG C3 offers more HDMI 2.1 ports, Dolby Vision gaming support, and a more comprehensive Game Optimizer menu. Choose based on whether you prioritize picture impact (S90C) or connectivity (C3).

faq.q: Is QD-OLED better than WOLED for TV picture quality?
faq.a: Neither is definitively better—they offer different strengths. QD-OLED (Samsung S90C) provides brighter images and more saturated colors, ideal for HDR and bright rooms. WOLED (LG C3) offers better viewing angles, more accurate colors, and more uniform brightness. Your preference depends on viewing environment, seating arrangement, and whether you prioritize vibrancy or accuracy.

meta_title: LG C3 vs Samsung S90C (2026): Which OLED TV Should You Buy?
meta_description: Detailed comparison of LG C3 vs Samsung S90C OLED TVs. We break down brightness, viewing angles, gaming features, and color technology to help you pick the right premium television.
